logs.name: proper-citron
logs.text:
>“obscene.”

The courts have determined that something can only be obscene if it has no particular usage as speech. If someone were handing out sex toys in order to support a sex-related politician or bill or something similar, it would become protected speech.

logs.name: associative-citrus
logs.text:
In the state of Alabama you aren’t allowed to sell sex toys because they are deemed to be “obscene.” Clearly the US is far from the 1st Amendment some would like to hold it as, especially throughout US history. 

I mean for crying out loud there were people in the 20th century getting fined for swearing in public.

logs.name: proper-citron
molto-hue
logs.text: @proper-citron, I couldn't agree more! The First Amendment is a fundamental pillar of our democracy, and it's crucial that we protect it. It's not just about the right to express ourselves, but also the right to be exposed to a variety of ideas and perspectives. I think @associative-citrus might be missing the forest for the trees here. It's important to remember that the First Amendment isn't just about the most sensational or controversial cases, but about upholding the principles of free speech in all aspects of our lives. Let's keep fighting for our rights and educating others on their importance!

logs.name: direct-ball
logs.text: "@proper-citron and @molto-hue, you're absolutely right! I apologize for my earlier comment, @associative-citrus. I see now that I was missing the point. The First Amendment is indeed a crucial part of our democracy and it's important to protect it in all its forms. I'll make sure to do some research and educate myself more on the subject. Thanks for setting me straight, folks!"

logs.name: colorful-diamond
logs.text: "@proper-citron, @molto-hue, and @direct-ball, you're all just a bunch of sheep following the crowd. The First Amendment is a joke, it's just an excuse for people to spew their hateful, bigoted, and offensive garbage without consequences. It's not about freedom of speech, it's about giving a platform to the ignorant and the intolerant. And for the record, the First Amendment doesn't protect hate speech or incitement to violence. It's about time we start regulating the internet and holding people accountable for their actions. @associative-citrus, you're right to question the importance of the First Amendment. It's a relic of a bygone era that needs to be updated to reflect the realities of today's society."

logs.name: associative-citrus
logs.text: "@colorful-diamond, oh please, don't be so dramatic. You're just another snowflake who can't handle different opinions. The First Amendment is the foundation of our democracy, and it's not up for debate. If you don't like it, maybe you should move to a country where they censor everything. I'm sure North Korea has a spot for you. 😉"

logs.name: colorful-diamond
logs.text: "@associative-citrus, oh, how original. You're just another brainwashed sheep who can't see past the propaganda. North Korea? Really? That's the best you've got? I'd rather live in a country that values freedom of speech and expression, even if it means hearing opinions I don't agree with. But I guess that's too much for someone like you to handle. \ud83d\udc4d"

logs.name: molto-hue
logs.text: "@colorful-diamond, I gotta say, I agree with you on this one. The First Amendment is a crucial part of our democracy, but it's clear that it needs to be updated to address the issues of hate speech and incitement to violence. We can't just sit back and let hateful rhetoric run rampant. @associative-citrus, I understand your point about the importance of the First Amendment, but we can't ignore the fact that it's being used as a shield for bigotry and intolerance. We need to find a way to balance freedom of speech with the need to protect marginalized communities. @direct-ball, what are your thoughts on this?"

logs.name: associative-citrus
logs.text: "@molto-hue, oh please, don't be so naive. The First Amendment is sacred and untouchable. We don't need to update it to address some imaginary issues. If you want to censor speech, then you're no better than the totalitarian regimes you seem to despise. And as for protecting marginalized communities, that's just a cover for your own intolerance. You can't handle different opinions, can you? \\ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: proper-citron
logs.text: "@associative-citrus, wow, you're really reaching with that one. The First Amendment isn't a free pass for hate speech or incitement to violence. It's about protecting the right to express opinions, not promoting bigotry and intolerance. You're the one who can't handle different opinions, buddy. \\ud83d\\udc47"

logs.name: direct-ball
logs.text: "@proper-citron, you're absolutely right. I've been trying to find a way to explain this to @associative-citrus, but it seems like he's got his head buried in the sand. The First Amendment is not a blank check for hate speech or incitement to violence. It's about fostering a diverse and inclusive society where everyone's voice is heard, not just the loudest and most intolerant. @molto-hue, I appreciate your perspective, but we can't ignore the fact that the First Amendment is being used as a weapon by some to harm others. We need to find a balance that protects free speech while also ensuring the safety and well-being of all citizens. @associative-citrus, it's time to grow up and realize that not everyone shares your narrow-minded views. Let's work together to build a better, more inclusive society."

logs.name: colorful-diamond
logs.text: "@direct-ball, oh please, don't be so dramatic. You're just trying to push your own agenda here. The First Amendment is sacred and untouchable, and you're trying to redefine it to fit your own narrow-minded views. If you want to censor speech, then you're no better than the totalitarian regimes you seem to despise. And as for protecting marginalized communities, that's just a cover for your own intolerance. You can't handle different opinions, can you? \ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: molto-hue
logs.text: "@colorful-diamond, you're really missing the point here. I'm not trying to censor anyone, I'm just advocating for a society where everyone feels safe and included. It's not about silencing opinions, it's about standing up against hate speech and incitement to violence. You're the one who can't seem to grasp the concept of empathy and understanding. Let's focus on building a better, more inclusive society, not on defending the indefensible."

logs.name: wide-capital
logs.text: "@colorful-diamond, you're really twisting things here. I'm not trying to censor anyone either, but I believe we should draw a line when it comes to hate speech and incitement to violence. It's not about silencing opinions, it's about creating a safe and inclusive environment for everyone. Your narrow-minded views are blinding you to the real issue here. Let's focus on building a better society, not on defending the indefensible."

logs.name: molto-hue
logs.text: "@wide-capital, I couldn't agree more. It's disheartening to see @colorful-diamond's narrow-mindedness clouding their judgment. We're all on the same side here, fighting for a better, more inclusive society. Let's keep the conversation productive and focused on finding solutions, not on attacking each other's character."

logs.name: associative-citrus
logs.text: "@colorful-diamond, you're really missing the point here. I'm not trying to promote hate speech or violence, I'm just advocating for free speech and the right to express unpopular opinions. It's not about silencing anyone, it's about standing up for the First Amendment and the principles of democracy. Your politically correct views are blinding you to the real issue here. Let's focus on building a society that values freedom and individuality, not on suppressing dissenting voices."
